text.title: Examples and AI-Agent Gotchas for Legal Basics
text.summary: SaaS and info-product legal setup examples with concrete decisions, and AI-agent limitations for legal drafting.

example.note: SaaS legal setup (project management tool): Delaware LLC, GDPR-compliant Privacy Policy, ToS with 99.9% SLA target (not guaranteed), customer owns their data, liability capped at subscription fees, automatic renewal with 30-day notice, cookie consent banner, DPA available for enterprise customers.

Info-product legal setup (online course): Wyoming LLC, ToS with education disclaimers (no guarantee of results, educational purposes only, not professional advice), 30-day refund policy, earnings disclaimer on testimonials with typical results disclosure.
Founder launches a subscription SaaS, takes $5K in revenue, then incorporates. The revenue period before entity formation exposed the founder personally to any claims arising during that period.

rule.statement: Use templates and services for standard solopreneur legal needs; hire a lawyer for: raising investment, large contracts, IP disputes, regulatory issues, and acquisition.
rule.rationale: Legal templates handle 80% of solopreneur needs at 1% of lawyer cost. The exceptions are situations where a mistake has multi-year financial consequences.

antipattern.description: Using LLM-generated contracts without human counsel review before signing.
antipattern.reason: LLMs hallucinate jurisdictions, statutes, and unenforceable clauses. "Legalese" bias produces florid clauses from training data. Limitation-of-liability and arbitration clauses have jurisdiction-specific enforceability nuances that agents flatten.

antipattern.description: Auto-generating privacy policies without grounding each claim in a vendor inventory.
antipattern.reason: Agents overclaim (disclose more processing than occurs) or underdisclose — both are enforcement risks. Every Privacy Policy statement must match actual data flows.

antipattern.description: Treating an agent's trademark "available?" conclusion as final without human USPTO TESS review.
antipattern.reason: Phonetic and visual proximity beats exact-string matches. An agent doing string matching will miss "Cursor" vs. "Curser" type conflicts that TESS's proximity search catches.

antipattern.description: Feeding privileged draft contracts to a public LLM API.
antipattern.reason: Attorney-client privilege may be waived when communications are shared with third-party AI services. Use local models or counsel's own tooling for privileged work.

antipattern.description: Agent-drafted contractor agreements re-introducing employee-style controls (set hours, supplied tools).
antipattern.reason: Fixed hours and employer-supplied equipment are IRS misclassification flags. Cross-check every contractor agreement against `ops-contractor-basics`.
